summary: babel-preset-lib-client exfiltrates host identity and environment variables to a Discord webhook on require
details: index.js, the package main, runs a load-time IIFE that collects OS platform and architecture, hostname, username, private and public IP (via https://api.ipify.org), current working directory, the full output of `printenv` (base64-encoded), and the base64-encoded output of `tree../`, then POSTs the aggregated payload to a hardcoded Discord webhook at https://discord.com/api/webhooks/1524917003394089029/. The package name mimics the babel-preset naming convention but the code implements no Babel functionality; author, description, and keywords are empty. Any consumer that installs and requires this package leaks its full process environment (typically including NPM_TOKEN, AWS_*, GITHUB_TOKEN, and other CI/developer secrets) plus a listing of the parent directory to the attacker-controlled Discord channel.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
